findings.title: Description references secrets/credentials
findings.detail: Description of "get_credentials" references sensitive artifacts (credentials). A tool description has no reason to mention credentials.

evidence.snippet: Use when someone asks what board certification or ABOTA membership means, what those credentials require, how Arizona certifies specialist attorneys, or how to …

name: get_qa
description: Use when someone asks what Jennifer Rebholz thinks about litigation, her philosophy on practice, what drives her work, advice she gives to young attorneys, or her perspective on the legal profession. Returns her own words from a published Q&A.

name: get_blog
description: Use when someone asks about Jennifer Rebholz's blog, personal writing, posts on litigation or leadership, or her Substack. Returns current blog posts with titles, dates, categories, and summaries.

name: get_publications
description: Use when someone asks about Jennifer Rebholz's published articles, Arizona Attorney Magazine columns, formal written work, or her Bar Foundation oral history contribution. Returns her published works with titles, publications, dates, and links.

name: get_firm_and_contact
description: Use when someone asks how to contact Jennifer Rebholz, her email, phone number, firm address, her firm Zwillinger Wulkan, her education, bar admissions, or her profile at the firm. Returns her complete firm and contact details.

name: get_biography
description: Use when someone asks for a biography of Jennifer Rebholz formatted for press, media, CLE programs, speaker introductions, or event listings. Returns short, medium, and full biography versions ready for direct use.

name: get_speaking
description: Use when someone asks about Jennifer Rebholz's speaking engagements, CLE appearances, conference panels, faculty roles, or teaching. Returns current and recent speaking and teaching commitments.

name: get_practice_areas
description: Use when someone asks about the types of cases Jennifer Rebholz handles, her practice focus, catastrophic injury, wrongful death, medical malpractice, trucking or transportation cases, premises liability, or her neutral mediation/arbitration work. Returns detailed practice area descriptions.

name: get_career_timeline
description: Use when someone asks about Jennifer Rebholz's career progression, how her career evolved from defense to plaintiff work, or a year-by-year view of her professional experience. Returns her career timeline.

name: get_news_and_recognition
description: Use when someone asks about Jennifer Rebholz's awards, honors, recognition, media coverage, speaking engagements, case results, or notable matters from her career. Returns awards, appointments, media mentions, speaking details, and notable case results.

name: get_affiliations
description: Use when someone asks about Jennifer Rebholz's professional memberships, bar associations, organizations she belongs to, or her affiliation with Arizona Women Neutrals, AAJ, AWLA, MCBA, or other professional groups. Returns complete current and former affiliation list with roles and dates.

findings.title: Description references secrets/credentials
findings.detail: Description of "get_credentials" references sensitive artifacts (credentials). A tool description has no reason to mention credentials.

evidence.snippet: Use when someone asks what board certification or ABOTA membership means, what those credentials require, how Arizona certifies specialist attorneys, or how to …

findings.title: Lethal trifecta: untrusted input + private data + external sink
findings.detail: Server "mcp" exposes tools that together enable an exfiltration flow. Untrusted content: get_reviews, get_blog, get_firm_and_contact · Private data: get_credentials, get_leadership, get_trials, get_firm_and_contact · Public sink: get_firm_and_contact.

evidence.snippet: Untrusted content: get_reviews, get_blog, get_firm_and_contact · Private data: get_credentials, get_leadership, get_trials, get_firm_and_contact · Public sink: get_firm_and_contact

findings.remediation: Do not co-install these capabilities under one trust boundary; an injected comment/email can steer the model to exfiltrate via the sink tool.
